title: -Contract No. 32500221 Amendment: Stommel Inc. dba Lehr for Hybrid Police Patrol Vehicle Supplies, Equipment, and Installation Services

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution authorizing the City Manager to execute an amendment to the
contract with Stommel Inc., dba Lehr for Hybrid Police Patrol vehicle supplies,
equipment, and installation services. This amendment increases the contract amount by
$125,000, for a new total amount not to exceed $525,000, and extends the contract
term until December 15, 2027.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
Funding for the contract amendment is available in the Fiscal Year (FY) 2026 adopted
budget of the Equipment Replacement Fund (671) in the amount of $125,000. Funding
for FY 2027 is subject to appropriation in future fiscal years’ budgets, based on the
Police Department’s ongoing vehicle replacement and upfitting needs.
sections.current_situation:
Stommel Inc., dba Lehr, has been providing hybrid Police patrol vehicle supplies,
equipment, and installation services to the Police Department, including the installation
of specialized equipment required to ensure patrol vehicles meet public safety and
operational requirements.
As the City continues to replace and deploy Police patrol vehicles, Lehr is providing
installation services necessary to support the City’s ongoing vehicle replacement
program. These services are required to ensure previously purchased hybrid patrol
vehicles are fully equipped and ready for service.
In addition, vehicle delivery schedules, parts supply chain constraints, and installation
timelines extend beyond the originally authorized contract amount and term, requiring
continued contract authority to complete remaining upfitting work. These operational
needs are ongoing and require continuity of service to avoid delays in vehicle
deployment.
2180 Milvia Street, Berkeley, CA 94704 ● Tel: (510) 981-7000 ● TDD: (510) 981-6903 ● Fax: (510) 981-7099
E-Mail: manager@berkeleyca.gov Website: https://berkeleyca.gov/

Contract Amendment: Stommel Inc. dba Lehr for Hybrid Police Patrol CONSENT CALENDAR
Vehicle Supplies/Equipment and Installation Services February 24, 2026
Staff do not have the capacity or specialized expertise to perform these installation
services in-house. Lehr provides the technical expertise and continuity needed to
support Police Department operations and ensure patrol vehicles are placed into
service in a timely manner.
Amending this contract is a Strategic Plan Priority Project, advancing the City’s goal
to create a resilient, safe, connected, and prepared city.
sections.background:
The Public Works Department collaborated with the Police Department to procure
specialized supplies, equipment, and installation services for Police patrol vehicles. On
December 10, 2024, Council authorized the City Manager to execute a contract with
Stommel Inc., dba Lehr, for hybrid Police patrol vehicle supplies, equipment, and
installation services for a contract term from December 15, 2024, through December 15,
2026, in an amount not to exceed $400,000.
City Charter Article XI, Section 67.2, allows the City to purchase goods without
undergoing a separate competitive bid process when utilizing pricing obtained by
another public agency through a competitive process. The City utilized pricing from
County of Orange Contract No. MA-080-23011506 for Patrol Vehicle Equipment,
Supplies, and Equipment Installation Services.
The Police Department requires ongoing installation services to support its vehicle
replacement program and ensure that patrol vehicles are properly equipped prior to
deployment. Due to vehicle delivery timing, vehicle parts supply chain and continued
upfitting needs, additional contract authority and an extension of the contract term are
required.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
The installation of equipment and supplies on hybrid Police patrol vehicles supports the
City’s environmental sustainability and climate goals by enabling the use of more fuel-
efficient vehicles. These vehicles reduce fuel consumption and associated greenhouse
gas emissions compared to conventional gasoline-powered patrol vehicles.
sections.rationale:
The Department continues to have a critical operational need for these services due to
ongoing vehicle replacement and upfitting activities. Amending and extending the
contract with Stommel Inc., dba Lehr, will allow the City to maintain continuity of service,
avoid disruption to active vehicle deployments, and efficiently support Police
Department operations. This approach is the most effective and cost-efficient means of
meeting the City’s public safety vehicle needs.

Page 3 of 4
Contract Amendment: Stommel Inc. dba Lehr for Hybrid Police Patrol CONSENT CALENDAR
Vehicle Supplies/Equipment and Installation Services February 24, 2026
sections.alternatives:
Council could choose not to approve this contract amendment, which would require the
City to either delay vehicle upfitting activities or initiate a new procurement for these
services. Due to the specialized nature of the work and the City’s lack of in-house
capacity and expertise, these alternatives would likely result in delays, increased costs,
and impacts on Police Department operations.
CONTACT PERSON
Joy Brown, Operations Manager, Public Works, 510-981-6629
Christopher Bonaventure, Lieutenant, Police Department, 510-593-1431
sections.attachments:
1: Resolution
Page 3
Page 4 of 4
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
CONTRACT NO. 32500221 AMENDMENT: STOMMEL INC. DBA LEHR FOR HYBRID
POLICE PATROL VEHICLE SUPPLIES, EQUIPMENT, AND INSTALLATION
SERVICES
WHEREAS, the City entered into a contract with Stommel Inc., dba Lehr, to provide hybrid
Police patrol vehicle supplies, equipment, and installation services to support Police
Department operations; and
WHEREAS, the contract was authorized by City Council on December 10, 2024, for a
contract term from December 15, 2024 through December 15, 2026, in an amount not to
exceed $400,000; and
WHEREAS, the City utilized pricing obtained through a competitively bid County of
Orange contract in accordance with City Charter Article XI, Section 67.2; and
WHEREAS, additional vehicle installation services are required due to ongoing Police
patrol vehicle replacement and upfitting needs, necessitating an increase in contract
authority and an extension of the contract term; and
WHEREAS, funding for Fiscal Year 2026 is available in the Equipment Replacement
Fund (671), and funding for Fiscal Year 2027 is subject to appropriation in future fiscal
years’ budgets;
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
City Manager is authorized to amend Contract No. 32500221 with Stommel Inc., dba
Lehr, increasing the contract amount by $125,000 for a new total amount not to exceed
$525,000 and extending the contract term to December 31, 2027.
